titleStmt.title: SII 41.389: original edition by S. Rajagopal
No. 389. (A.R. No. 604 of 1905.) Tiṅgaḷūr, Erode Taluk, Coimbatore District. On the south and east base of central shrine in the Chandramaulīśvara temple at Tiṅgaḷūr.

summary.p: Built in at the beginning. The king made a gift of a tank with its cultivable land for celebrating a festival called Kulōttuṅga-chōḻan sandi using one third of the cultivator's share (kuḍimakkaḷvāram) of the produce of the land.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
koṉerinmaikoṇṭān kuṟuppunāṭṭiṟ tiṅkaḷūr Āḷuṭaiyār cantirapuravicuramuṭaiyār koyiliṟ tevarkaṉmikaḷukku nammolai kuṭuttapaṭiyāvatu Iṉṉāyaṉāṟku Amutupaṭi kolottuṅkacoḻan cantiyāka Amutu ceytaruḷukaikku nāṅkuṭutta kuḷam Ikkoyilukku vaṭakkiṟ tāmaraikkuḷam Ikkuḷattiṉkiṭpuṉcey viḷaitta kuṭimakkaḷe yikkuḷattāl nirpāyumiṭam viḷaittuk kuṭiniṅkāttevartāṉamāka Uḻutu viḷaitta nilattukku muṉṟattoṉṟuvāraṅ kaḷattiley kaṇkāṇittu Aḷavukoḷḷa Eṭuttikkuṭuppārkaḷāka
ṟṟuḷḷiṉa kuṭimakkaḷ vārattiley kuṭuttuvaruvatākavum .
Digital edition of SII 41.389 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
